title: 'Clear breach of trust': Iran closes Hormuz again over Israel attacks on Lebanon
content: Before the closure, ships seeking passage through the strait while the interim agreement remained in force were required to submit transit requests at least 48 hours before arrival. Meanwhile, United States vice president JD Vance said he was confident the ceasefire could hold, adding that there was no evidence Iran was closing the Strait of Hormuz.

title: India break 22-year-old ODI record in just 6 balls against Afghanistan
content: India launched their chase against Afghanistan with a record-shattering 23 runs in the opening over, fueled by Yashasvi Jaiswal's aggressive batting and several extras. This remarkable start, following a five-run penalty against Afghanistan, set the tone for the chase. Earlier, Afghanistan posted 218, with captain Hashmatullah Shahidi scoring a century, but were undone by Prasidh Krishna's stellar 5-wicket haul.

title: Man beaten to death while trying to board train at Delhi's Shahdara railway station
content: A tragic incident unfolded at Shahdara railway station as a 32-year-old man, Pankaj Dhama, was allegedly beaten to death by fellow passengers during a chaotic rush to board the Yoga Express. The altercation, which began on platform number 3, escalated into a fatal assault. Authorities are actively investigating, reviewing CCTV footage and questioning witnesses to identify and apprehend those responsible for the deadly scuffle.

title: Trump on Anthropic CEO’s response to Claude ban: People get put in prison immediately
content: Former President Donald Trump has expressed confidence in AI firm Anthropic and its CEO, Dario Amodei, following White House concerns over advanced AI models. Trump stated Amodei responded "very responsibly" to national security worries, a shift from earlier apprehension. Restrictions on certain AI models have been eased as discussions progress on security standards, with Trump emphasizing the need for US AI leadership over hindering innovation.

title: The 'curse' of Bhangarh: Science or supernatural?
content: The allure of Bhangarh Fort, India's famed 'haunted' site, is explored beyond ghost tales, revealing a blend of history, psychology, and science. Built in the 17th century by Raja Madho Singh, this striking Aravalli landmark's supposed hauntings are debunked by locals. They attribute post-sunset dangers to wild animals, not spectral residents, urging a look at the fort's rich past.

title: Monsoon, El Niño and market trends: NSE highlights key risks for India’s 2026 economy
content: India's 2026 economic outlook hinges on monsoon performance, with El Niño posing a significant risk of deficient rainfall impacting agriculture. Meanwhile, the nation's equity investor base is rapidly expanding, becoming younger and more diverse, with a notable surge in participation from smaller cities. Despite this growth, trading activity remains heavily concentrated among a small group of large investors across all market segments.

title: Bizarre scene! Gurbaz kicks ball to boundary, gifts Rohit four runs - Watch
content: Afghanistan wicketkeeper Rahmanullah Gurbaz provided a bizarre moment during the third ODI against India when a top edge from Rohit Sharma bounced off his knees and ran away for four, leaving fans amused. Gurbaz appeared to hurt himself while attempting the save but resumed play after treatment. Afghanistan later posted 218, with Hashmatullah Shahidi scoring a century.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: US mass shooting: 12 injured after 2 gunmen open fire on Chicago street crowd
content: Chicago witnessed a horrific mass shooting on Saturday when two individuals opened fire from an SUV, injuring at least 12 people. The incident, which occurred on Juneteenth, saw victims aged 17 to 47 rushed to hospitals. This violence adds to a disturbing trend, with over 21 people shot and four fatalities reported in the city since Friday evening.

title: Samantha Ruth Prabhu expecting her first child with Raj Nidimoru
content: Actress Samantha Ruth Prabhu is reportedly expecting her first child with husband Raj. The news follows the massive success of her latest film, 'Maa Inti Bangaaram,' which reportedly recouped its investment on release day. While Samantha and Raj haven't officially announced, a spokesperson has confirmed the pregnancy. Fans have been speculating after spotting what appeared to be a baby bump in recent photos.

title: EU removes negative travel advisory for Assam; Himanta says talks on with US, Canada
content: Assam's Chief Minister announced that the European Union has removed its negative travel advisories for the state, a move that signifies a strong vote of confidence. This development, following a recent EU diplomat visit, excludes three districts still under AFSPA. The state is now engaging with the US and Canada to encourage similar revisions to their travel guidance.

title: White House clears rule limiting status of foreign students in US that many have opposed
content: The White House has greenlit a new rule from the Department of Homeland Security that will end the open-ended stay for foreign students, exchange visitors, and media representatives.  Instead, these individuals will now receive a fixed four-year admission period, requiring them to renew their status. This change aims to reduce overstays and enhance national security, though critics warn of administrative burdens.

title: Why did India start at 5/0 vs Afghanistan? Costly blunder explained
content: India's chase against Afghanistan began with a surprising 5/0 score, thanks to a five-run penalty awarded after Afghan captain Hashmatullah Shahidi repeatedly ran on the protected pitch area.  Despite this, Shahidi scored a maiden ODI century, guiding Afghanistan to 218.  Prasidh Krishna was the star bowler, taking a career-best 5/23.

title: IND vs AFG: Krishna scripts history in Chennai, becomes first Indian ever to...
content: Prasidh Krishna starred with a career-best 5 for 23, becoming the first Indian to take an ODI five-wicket haul in Chennai and setting a new venue record. His early burst reduced Afghanistan to 36/4, but captain Hashmatullah Shahidi fought back with a maiden ODI century as the visitors recovered to post 218 before being bowled out.

title: Trump drops 'Indo' from the name of Pacific Command, what does this name change mean for India
content: US President Donald Trump has reportedly reverted the US Indo-Pacific Command's name back to Pacific Command, a significant shift for the military's oldest and largest combatant command. This development comes as India and the US deepen their strategic partnership, marked by increased defense cooperation and joint exercises aimed at regional stability and countering emerging challenges. High-level visits underscore India's growing alignment with US security frameworks.

title: Milk prices may rise again by July as El Niño threatens fodder supply
content: Dairy consumers might face another price hike by July or August, with industry experts warning of a potential 3-4% increase. This follows a recent 2-3% rise in May. Concerns are mounting over El Niño potentially worsening fodder shortages and impacting milk production, prompting the Maharashtra government to urge farmers to boost fodder cultivation. Major dairies are closely monitoring the situation.

title: 5 killed, 30 injured after under-construction temple roof collapses in Maharashtra
content: Tragedy struck Parbhani as the roof of an under-construction Hanuman temple collapsed, claiming five lives and injuring at least 17 devotees. The incident occurred at Yashwadi in Manavat taluka on Saturday afternoon. Heavy Rajasthani pink stones, used in construction, are suspected to be a factor. The temple was reportedly open despite ongoing work, following local requests. Rescue operations are underway, with authorities promising financial aid to affected families.

title: India's urban housing demand set for a major boost - here's what's driving it
content: India's urban landscape is poised for a major transformation, driven by rapid urbanization and policy reforms. A KPMG report highlights the critical role of housing and urban planning in achieving "Viksit Bharat 2047." Key focus areas include expanding affordable housing for lower-income groups and developing a structured rental housing market. The report advocates for streamlined approvals, improved infrastructure, and enhanced regulatory coordination to ensure sustainable urban growth and protect homebuyers.

title: India-US trade deal: Piyush Goyal reveals what's holding up the pact
content: India and the US have finalized the framework for a trade agreement, but its implementation hinges on New Delhi securing a competitive tariff advantage over rival nations. Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India's duties must be lower than those of competing countries before the deal can be enacted. This tariff competitiveness is the key sticking point, despite US Ambassador Sergio Gor expressing confidence that the agreement is nearing completion.

title: NEET centre mix-up: NTA says Nasik candidate himself selected Abu Dhabi option
content: Highlighting the NTA's web-activity records, the test-conducting body said its record indicated that the city was changed through "candidate's own registered login credentials" during the open correction window. The NTA also claimed that the centre was previewed twice from the candidates' credentials. "​Despite that NTA has accorded to aspirant’s request and the change of centre was actioned," the body said. ​​

title: After 4644 days, Afghanistan captain Shahidi finally gets first ODI century
content: Afghanistan captain Hashmatullah Shahidi achieved a significant milestone, scoring his maiden ODI century with a resilient 102 against India. This landmark hundred came after a remarkable 4,644-day wait since his debut, placing him among the longest-waiting players for their first ODI ton. Shahidi's determined innings, battling through trouble and cramps, guided Afghanistan to a respectable 218 after an early collapse.

title: Hum do, hamare 1.9: What India’s falling fertility rates reveal
content: India, once concerned about a population boom, now faces a declining birth rate, with the Total Fertility Rate dropping to 1.9 children per woman, below the replacement level. This significant shift reflects women's increased educational attainment, career focus, and later marriages, leading to more personal choices about family planning. The narrative is evolving from population control to individual decisions, marking a societal transition.

title: 'Beat her over petty issues': Pune techie jumps to death; husband, in-law booked
content: A 27-year-old software engineer tragically ended her life by jumping from her Pune residence on Thursday. Her brother's complaint alleges that her husband and mother-in-law subjected her to persistent harassment and physical abuse over minor matters.  The couple, who met at a software firm, married in November 2024. Police have registered a case against the husband and his relatives for abetting the suicide.

title: Rohit joins Ganguly, Raina in elite ODI list, becomes fourth Indian to ...
content: Captain Rohit Sharma achieved a rare fielding milestone in Chennai, becoming the fourth Indian to be involved in the first three dismissals of an ODI innings. He took all three catches at first slip against Afghanistan, marking a spectacular start to the match. This remarkable feat adds another chapter to his illustrious career as India aims for a series whitewash.

title: Union minister Bandi Sanjay's son gets bail in POCSO case
content: The case was registered at the Petbasheerabad police station on May 8 under relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and the POCSO Act based on a complaint filed by the mother of a 17-year-old girl. According to the complaint, Bageerath was in a relationship with the minor and had allegedly subjected her to sexual harassment. After investigators recorded the girl's statement, additional and more serious charges under the BNS and POCSO Act were incorporated into the case.

title: Why India doesn’t rank in top 50 world’s most powerful passports
content: Despite India's robust economic growth, its passport ranks surprisingly low globally, hovering around the 80th position. This reflects a slower expansion of visa-free access compared to other nations. Factors like diplomatic ties, reciprocal agreements, and perceived security influence rankings, not just economic might. India's progress is gradual, requiring strategic diplomatic efforts and strengthened internal systems to boost international trust and improve its travel freedom.

title: Happy Father's Day: Top 80 wishes, greetings and quotes to make your dad feel special
content: Fathers often express love through actions, not words, and Father's Day on June 21, 2026, is a time to appreciate their guidance and support. Simple gestures like cooking a favorite meal or sharing memories can mean more than gifts. This day is about acknowledging their sacrifices and letting them know they are deeply appreciated.

title: Visa rejected, Pakistan captain Butt likely to miss FIH Pro League
content: Pakistan's hockey team faces a major blow as captain Ammad Shakeel Butt is denied a visa for the upcoming FIH Pro League matches in England due to a past overstay. Head coach Manzur-ul-Hasan also awaits clearance. This comes as the team struggles, having lost all 11 matches and facing potential relegation, adding to pre-World Cup concerns.

title: First time in 1078-ODI history! Rohit Sharma and Prasidh become first ...
content: Prasidh Krishna and Rohit Sharma etched their names in ODI history as India began their final match against Afghanistan with a bang. Krishna claimed the first three wickets of the innings, all caught by Sharma at first slip. This remarkable bowler-fielder partnership marked a unique first for India in over a thousand ODI matches, setting a dominant tone for the hosts.

title: AIFF weighs club-led ISL model, considers renaming itself Football Federation of Bharat
content: AIFF has deferred a decision on the proposed club-led ISL model until next week, while also considering changing its name to Football Federation of Bharat. The ISL is scheduled to begin on September 4.

title: Paresh Ganatra recalls being replaced by Paresh Rawal in 'Aankhen'
content: Paresh Ganatra reveals he was initially slated to play Paresh Rawal's role in the cult hit 'Aankhen,' based on his Gujarati play 'Aandhro Paato.' When Amitabh Bachchan joined the project, the film's scale changed, leading to Ganatra taking on the character of Shailesh. This role, though smaller, was well-received and paved the way for his subsequent work in films like 'No Entry.'

title: 'She wants to be friends again': Trump repeats Meloni asked for photo 'over and over' at G7
content: Trump, apparently miffed by being given a cold shoulder by European allies during the Iran war, said Meloni's popularity has taken a dip ever since she refused the US access to Italy's landing strips or runways, which caused "a great logistical inconvenience" to American operations. The remarks came after Meloni accused Trump of fabricating a story that she had "begged" him to take a photograph with her during the recent G7 summit.

title: A $60 billion windfall? How US-Iran deal could revive Tehran's oil industry
content: The easing of restrictions to roll back a sanctions regime that was built over more than a decade to isolate Iran economically and curb its nuclear ambitions. The move could allow Tehran to expand sales to a wider pool of buyers after years of relying largely on discounted shipments through opaque trading networks, primarily to independent refiners in China.

title: Death trap in the deep: The silent sinkhole of Bengaluru’s sewage plant
content: Two workers vanished inside a Bengaluru sewage plant, sparking outrage from activists who claim they were illegally hired for hazardous work without safety. The water authority denies manual scavenging, stating the men entered unauthorizedly while attempting gate repairs. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about worker safety and accountability in the city's sanitation sector, with calls for prosecution of responsible officials.

title: Court warns unchecked police brutality could turn force 'like Nazi Germany'
content: Patna High Court has ordered an FIR against a Buxar SHO for allegedly breaking a man's legs due to his caste. The victim's complaints were ignored by local police. The court rejected the police's claim of an accidental fall, citing X-ray evidence and the victim's fear. The investigation is now transferred to the CID, with the court emphasizing equal application of law and warning against unchecked police brutality.

title: Karnataka youth assault by 'right-wing activists' over lunch with Hindu girlfriend
content: A young man has lodged a complaint with Hubballi rural police, alleging he was assaulted by suspected Right-wing activists for having lunch with his Hindu girlfriend. The group reportedly demanded Aadhaar cards, then assaulted him after he identified as Christian. They also allegedly took his girlfriend's video, made false accusations, and threatened the couple's mother for a ransom.

title: Cross-voting unfortunate, high command will take strict action, says Joshi
content: Terming the cross-voting in the Legislative Council election in the State as ‘unfortunate’, Union Minister Pralhad Joshi says that the BJP high command has taken the issue seriously and will take stern action

title: Naidu pats tenant farmer for adopting natural farming methods
content: The Chief Minister says that farmers such as Nagabhushanam are role models for sustainable agriculture; he also buys a litre of cold-pressed groundnut oil after obtaining his wife’s approval over phone

title: Srisailam temple launches drive to plant 5,000 saplings for conservation
content: Participating in the plantation drive, Board of Trustees chairman says saplings will be planted on both sides of the ring road

title: Cockroach Janta Party protest LIVE | Delhi police denies permission to continue, Abhijeet Dipke says won't move
content: Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke said the protest demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan will continue

title: AKSTU takes out Secretariat march
content: Raising a slew of demands, including that the State withdraw from the PM SHRI scheme and corrective measures to address what it described as the neglect of the general education sector in the revised Budget

title: Sabari rail action council flays omission of project in Budget, demands ₹200-crore allocation
content: The Sabari Railway Action Council Federation termed the omission disappointing and alleged that the Revised Budget had pushed the project back into uncertainty

title: Minister calls on students to combat plastic usage in A.P.
content: Participating in the ‘Swarna Andhra - Swachha Andhra’ programme in Nandyal, Janardhan Reddy says government is organising ‘Swachh Divas’ across the State on the third Saturday of every month

title: Akal Takht releases video of Mann’s appearance before it over ‘objectionable’ video
content: Akal Takht said that when the Chief Minister appeared at Akal Takht Sahib; he addressed the matter regarding an objectionable video and had said, "This is completely fake."

title: 10 lakh people expected to join Yoga Day events across Karnataka
content: State-level programme at Vidhana Soudha to draw 2,000 participants; AYUSH Department launches year-long ‘Nitya Yoga’ initiative

title: Ensure there are no 'glitches' this time: Rahul to government ahead of NEET-UG re-exam
content: I hope you have a wonderful, excellent exam and you all do really, really well. Remember, we are with you, we want to look after you, protect you and make sure that you have a brilliant future, says Congress leader Rahul Gandhi

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | IMD warns of isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall in several regions
content: Isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall likely over sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim and northeast India during the week with possibility of isolated extremely heavy rainfall over sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim till June 21

title: NEET re-exam: NTA issues safety advisory; sends WhatsApp updates regarding admit cards
content: "NTA is sending reminder SMS and emails to candidates to download their admit cards for the re-examination on Sunday, 21 June 2026," the agency said in a public advisory

title: Kochi Corporation to advance Digital Twin project in bursts of five divisions at a time
content: The initiative, implemented by UL Technology Solutions (ULTS) has already been completed in eight area-based development divisions identified under the Smart City project during the pilot phase

title: Kerala Police rope in religious leaders for anti-drug drive ‘Operation Toofan’
content: Home Minister Chennithala declares Samastha Kerala Jem-iyyathul Ulama State president Syed Mohammed Jifri Muthukoya Thangal and Sunni leader Kanthapuram A.P. Aboobacker Musliar as ‘Toofan Warriors’

title: MES leaders, activists stopped from meeting Union Minister Amit Shah
content: Activists of Maharashtra Ekikaran Samiti (MES), who were proceeding to Kolhapur to meet Union Minister Amit Shah, allegedly to complain about ‘injustice’ to Marathi speakers, were stopped by the Belagavi police on Saturday

title: Great Nicobar project will boost India's economic growth: Kiren Rijiju
content: Great Nicobar Project will be a 'kendrabindoo' when it comes to our economic growth. It will be a major maritime and logistics hub in the Indo-Pacific region, says Union Minister Kiren Rijiju

title: Seven dead, 25 injured in roof collapse at temple in Maharashtra’s Parbhani
content: The rescue operations continued for two and a half hours; cause of the collapse is under investigation

title: Kerala’s former Excise Minister flays UDF for reducing tax on low-alcohol drinks
content: He says the previous LDF government permitted production of low-alcohol beverages only from fruits and farm produce, while the UDF government is allowing production using spirit, which will cause corruption and social harm

title: Truck, milk tanker collision sparks fire
content: A maize-laden truck and a milk tanker were gutted in a fire that was caused by a head-on collision between the two in Belagavi district on Saturday

title: Rejection of chilli consignments by China raises concern among exporters, farmers in Andhra Pradesh
content: With the consignments containing traces of Methamidophos, exporters urge government to strengthen monitoring mechanisms and promote scientific pesticide usage; Spices Board in Guntur has been conducting post-harvest quality improvement programmes for the farmers to enhance export quality, says Deputy Director Anan Debbarma

title: NCP leader Padamsinh Patil, seven others acquitted in Nimbalkar murder case
content: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis and Deputy CM Eknath Shinde requested Union Home Minister Amit Shah to look into the matter; the CBI is slated to challenge the verdict in the Bombay High Court

title: ₹110 crore disbursed to 1.64 lakh farmers in Annamayya district
content: Addressing farmers during the launch of ‘Annadatha Sukhibhava-PM Kisan’ programme, Transport Minister says coalition government has been implementing welfare measures and reducing cultivation costs through subsidies on urea, drip irrigation and other inputs

title: Centre bans 16 fixed dose combination drugs, including antibiotics, painkillers, skin meds
content: Fixed dose combinations are medicines with two or more active drugs combined in a fixed ratio in a single dosage; expert review shows that some pose safety risks or have unproven therapeutic effects

title: Revanth in New Delhi, likely to raise SBI land row with RBI and the Centre
content: Senior government sources claimed Mr. Revanth Reddy believes there is a political dimension to the controversy and suspects that leaders of the Bharat Rashtra Samithi are attempting to exploit the issue to tarnish Telangana’s image among investors

title: PM-VBRY aimed at encouraging employees, employers: Mahesh Tenginakai
content: Mahesh Tenginakai, Hubballi-Dharwad Central MLA, says that the Pradhan Mantri Vikasit Bharat Rojgar Yojna (PM- VBRY) has been launched by the Union government to encourage first-time employees and also employers to boost formal job creation

title: SYS leader’s remarks on football frenzy spark social media debate
content: Abdussamad Pookkottur criticises “excessive enthusiasm of Muslim youth” during the ongoing FIFA World Cup, urges youngsters to prioritise their spending

title: Government clears 55 projects worth ₹7,506 crore, to create over 28,000 jobs in Karnataka
content: Of these, 41 projects would be located outside Bengaluru Urban and Bengaluru Rural districts, as part of the State’s Beyond Bengaluru initiative

title: MP engages children as anganwadi teacher, promotes cleanliness drive in Nandyal
content: Inspecting two anganwadi centres at Kothapalli, Byreddy Shabari urges parents to utilise the facilities provided by the government for their children’s health and educational development

title: NEET exam day checklist 2026: Dress code, documents and entry guidelines
content: Here is a comprehensive guide detailing the necessary items, documents, and entry requirements that NEET candidates should prepare.

title: Chittoor district to rationalise select polling stations for voter access, convenience, says DRO
content: Addressing representatives of political parties at the Collectorate, Mr. Mohan Kumar says SIR will help remove bogus and deceased voters and prepare a transparent electoral roll

title: Mob Mistakes NGO Worker For Kidnapper In Odisha Village, Thrashes, Strips Her
content: The woman and her colleague talked to children and distributed biscuits. Soon, a rumour spread in the village that they were child kidnappers.

title: Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz Again Over Israel's Attacks On Lebanon
content: Iran said that the closure was the "first step" in response to what it described as breaches of commitments.

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: 'Breach Of Deal': Iran Says Hormuz Closed Again Over Israel Attacking Lebanon
content: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: "Recording Vindicates Bhagwant Mann": AAP As Akal Takh Standoff Escalates
content: The Akal Takht released a recording of a coversation between its Jathedar and the Punjab chief minister.

title: "Asked Over And Over For Picture": Trump Rebuts Meloni's Didn't "Beg" Claim
content: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump said that Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni asked him for a photo "over and over" during the G7 Summit in France. He claimed that Meloni is resorting to these tactics because she is "doing poorly" in Ital

title: 'No Press Freedom': JD Vance Blames Pak For Delay In Releasing Iran Agreement
content: Speaking in a podcast called 'Interesting Times with Ross Douthat', US Vice President JD Vance said that although the US wanted to release the agreement on June 15, they could only do so two days later.

title: 'Circumstances Changed': IUML Parts Ways With DMK After Nearly 60 Years
content: The rise of Chief Minister Vijay's TVK in Tamil Nadu forced many parties to change their strategies and alliance calculations.

title: Pakistan Interior Minister Lands In Iran After Switzerland Talks Postponed
content: Tehran and Washington were due to hold talks in Switzerland on Friday, after signing a memorandum of understanding ending the war in the Middle East, but the latest negotiations have been postponed with no new date announced.

title: Costs Rising, Karnataka Bus Fare Hike Proposal Back On Table: Sources
content: Sources said the recent 12.5% salary hike for employees is expected to impose an additional financial burden of Rs 873.64 crore on the transport corporations.

title: 'No Power Can Match AIADMK-TVK Cadre Unity in Tamil Nadu': Aadhav Arjuna
content: "AIADMK cadres are disheartened. That is why they have started moving towards TVK," the minister said.

title: 'Thinking Of Doing Something Different': NEET Aspirant Dies By Suicide
content: According to police, the victim, identified as Jatin Kumar, was a resident of H-Block in Vijay Nagar and had been preparing for the medical entrance examination.

title: Assam Police Foil Bid To Smuggle Endangered Golden Langurs, 9 Arrested
content: In a major crackdown on wildlife trafficking, the Special Task Force (STF) of Assam Police conducted a successful operation in Assam's Chirang district's sidli area, dismantling an alleged international wildlife trafficking network.

title: Marijuana Plants Discovered Inside Kerala Temple Premises, Probe Launched
content: The two plants were found near the temple's covered walkway within the compound and were detected as part of the Excise Department's ongoing "Operation Thunder" narcotics crackdown.

title: Tamil Nadu Stops Use Of Temple Funds For Marriage Halls, Commercial Complexes
content: Sanction has been revoked for 29 marriage hall projects worth Rs 115.77 crore and 17 commercial complex projects worth Rs 130.08 crore.

title: Railways Runs Special Trains For NEET Retest, But Return Journey Is Before Exam
content: Lakhs of students are appearing for the NEET (UG) 2026 re-test after the May 3 exam was cancelled amid allegations of a paper leak.

title: India To Host BRICS Security Advisers' Meet With Focus On Cyber Threats
content: According to the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), the meeting will centre on the theme, "Non-traditional security challenges confronting the world today."

title: 'Foreign Degree Is Rs 1 Crore Debt': Is Studying Abroad Still Worth The Price?
content: A weaker rupee and rising tuition fees are forcing Indian families to ask a difficult question: does an overseas degree still deliver the returns?

title: After Algae, Peeling Paint Adds To Donald Trump's Pool Project Problems
content: The pool repainting project, which US media have reported cost around $14 million, is part of Trump's drive to put his stamp on Washington.

title: 20 Years, 128 Witnesses Later, Congress Leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar's Murder Accused Acquitted
content: 20 Years, 128 Witnesses Later, Maharashtra Congress Leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar's Murder Accused Acquitted
